records.ety: [OF. offendre, L. offendere, offensum; ob (see Ob-) + fendere (in comp.) to thrust, dash. See Defend.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Offended; p. pr. & vb. n. Offending.]

defs: To strike against; to attack; to assail.
To displease; to make angry; to affront.
quotes: A brother offended is harder to be won than a strong city.

defs: To be offensive to; to harm; to pain; to annoy; as, strong light offends the eye; to offend the conscience.
To transgress; to violate; to sin against.
quotes: Marry, sir, he hath offended the law.

defs: To oppose or obstruct in duty; to cause to stumble; to cause to sin or to fall.
quotes: Who hath you misboden or offended.
If thy right eye offend thee, pluck it out . . . And if thy right hand offend thee, cut it off.
Great peace have they which love thy law, and nothing shall offend them.

defs: To transgress the moral or divine law; to commit a crime; to stumble; to sin.
quotes: Whosoever shall keep the whole law, and yet offend in one point, he is guilty of all.
If it be a sin to covet honor, I am the most offending soul alive.

defs: To cause dislike, anger, or vexation; to displease.
quotes: I shall offend, either to detain or give it.
